channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: Elevated error rates for GPT 5.5 in Codex
item.description: Status: MonitoringWe have applied the mitigation and are monitoring the recovery.Affected components Codex Web (Degraded performance) CLI (Degraded performance) App (Degraded performance) VS Code extension (Degraded performance) Codex API (Degraded performance)

item.title: Incident with Webhooks
item.description: Jun 11, 20:00 UTC Monitoring - The degradation affecting Webhooks has been mitigated. We are monitoring to ensure stability. Jun 11, 19:58 UTC Update - We have applied a mitigation and are monitoring for recovery. Jun 11, 19:42 UTC Update - We are currently experiencing delays in Web Hook delivery...

item.title: Billing Invoice UI issue
item.description: Jun 11, 18:12 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare is investigating a Billing Dashboard UI issue. Some customers are not able to see their invoices for the last 3 months. The invoices exist in our system and automatic billing is not impacted.

item.title: Elevated errors across models
item.description: Jun 11, 17:56 UTC Resolved - The issue affecting various models has been resolved. Impact occurred from 09:42am PT / 16:42 UTC to 10:03am PT / 17:03 UTC. Jun 11, 17:27 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 11, 16:54 UTC Investigating - We are currently...

item.title: New Attacks Trick OpenClaw AI Agent Into Running Code and Leaking Secrets
item.description: Two security teams have shown, in separate research published this week, that OpenClaw, the popular self-hosted AI agent, can be driven to run attacker-controlled code or hand over sensitive data through ordinary-looking inputs. Imperva buried instructions inside shared contacts, vCards, and...

item.title: New GreatXML Exploit Bypasses Windows BitLocker via Recovery Partition XML Files
item.description: Security researcher Chaotic Eclipse (aka Nightmare-Eclipse and MSNightmare) has released a new Windows BitLocker bypass dubbed GreatXML, a day after they published an exploit for Microsoft Defender. "This was an accidental discovery, it took a total of 4 hours to find this," the researcher said in...

item.title: Workers Observability query failures
item.description: Jun 11, 16:09 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jun 11, 15:52 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 11, 15:39 UTC Identified - Cloudflare has identified an issue impacting some customers where Workers Observability queries may be returning...

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Opus 4.6
item.description: Jun 11, 15:01 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jun 11, 14:58 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 11, 14:37 UTC Identified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. Jun 11, 14:37 UTC Investigating - We are currently...

item.title: Disruption in service availabilty for Free and Go users
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Conversations (Operational)

item.title: Hackers Exploit Langflow Vulnerability for Remote Code Execution
item.description: Disclosed in March, the security defect enables unauthenticated attackers to write files to arbitrary locations on the system. The post Hackers Exploit Langflow Vulnerability for Remote Code Execution appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Siemens Says Desigo CC Files Flagged as Malware by Security Engines
item.description: A PowerShell script included in patch files appears to be triggering false positives by multiple security engines. The post Siemens Says Desigo CC Files Flagged as Malware by Security Engines appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: AI Broke Vulnerability Management. That's Why CISOs Are Moving Budget to BAS.
item.description: For thirty years, vulnerability management ran on a buffer: the months between when a vulnerability was found and when someone could figure out how to weaponize it. The solution was straightforward enough; triage by severity, schedule the fix, validate, and move on. The buffer was what made that...

item.title: FBI Seizes 13 Websites That Officials Say Were Used by China to Target and Recruit US Workers
item.description: The 13 websites purported to be affiliated with consulting companies that advertised job openings for current and former holders of security clearances The post FBI Seizes 13 Websites That Officials Say Were Used by China to Target and Recruit US Workers appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Google DeepMind is worried about what happens when millions of agents start to interact
item.description: Google DeepMind is funding research into the potential dangers of situations where millions of different AI agents interact with each other online. According to Rohin Shah, who directs the company’s AGI safety and alignment research, the mass-market arrival of agents that can carry out tasks...

item.title: Splunk, Palo Alto Networks Patch Severe Vulnerabilities
item.description: The security defects could allow attackers to create or modify arbitrary files and access and modify protected resources. The post Splunk, Palo Alto Networks Patch Severe Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Job titles of the future: Nature’s drug designer
item.description: In 2018, after nearly two decades working in Big Pharma, chemist Tim Cernak was ready to put his skills to a new use.  For Merck, he’d developed precision therapies for cancer, HIV, and diabetes that could target disease while minimizing harm to healthy cells. But as a lifelong nature lover,...

item.title: Inside soccer’s data renaissance
item.description: Imagine tuning in to the opening kickoff of a World Cup match and seeing a player intentionally send the ball all the way down the pitch and right out of bounds on the opponent’s end. Casual fans might scratch their heads. Where’s the logic in surrendering possession seconds into a game? If you...

item.title: ‘GreatXML’ Zero-Day Exploit Bypasses BitLocker
item.description: The PoC exploits Microsoft Defender’s offline scan to spawn a SYSTEM shell when rebooting in Recovery Mode. The post ‘GreatXML’ Zero-Day Exploit Bypasses BitLocker appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: OceanLotus Hits Vietnam Investors With SPECTRALVIPER in FireAnt Attack
item.description: The Vietnam-aligned threat actor known as OceanLotus has been attributed to two distinct campaigns that targeted domestic entities and stock investors with a backdoor known as SPECTRALVIPER. The campaigns involve a prolonged cyber espionage operation aimed at a Vietnamese infrastructure and...

item.title: Xiaomi's Next-Gen Xuanjie O3 Chip Expected to Go Head-to-Head with Qualcomm Snapdragon 8E5
item.description: Xiaomi's Next-Gen Xuanjie O3 Chip Expected to Go Head-to-Head with Qualcomm Snapdragon 8E5 Xiaomi is gearing up for its most ambitious foray into in-house chip...

item.title: Taiwan Moves to Criminalize Unauthorized AI Chip Exports to Mainland China in Major Policy Shift
item.description: Taiwan Moves to Criminalize Unauthorized AI Chip Exports to Mainland China in Major Policy Shift Taiwan authorities are preparing to impose sweeping new restri...

item.title: Huawei and Lenovo to Raise Prices in July as Chip Cost Pressures Roil Supply Chain
item.description: Huawei and Lenovo to Raise Prices in July as Chip Cost Pressures Roil Consumer Electronics Supply Chain Huawei and Lenovo have both announced price increases s...

item.title: China's LLM Showdown: Why Coding and Office Productivity Decide the Winner
item.description: China's LLM Showdown: Why Coding and Office Productivity Decide the Winner Chinese large language model companies have achieved something remarkable. Models li...

item.title: Xingyuanzhi Robot Raises ¥1 Billion in 10 Months for Embodied AI Brain Technology
item.description: Xingyuanzhi Robot Raises ¥1 Billion in 10 Months, Puts Its Chips on the 'Embodied Brain' Beijing-based Xingyuanzhi Robot has raised a total of 1 billion yuan (...

item.title: GitHub to Disable npm Install Scripts by Default to Stop Supply Chain Attacks
item.description: GitHub has announced what it said are "breaking changes" coming to npm version 12, one of which turns off install scripts by default to combat software supply chain threats. The changes aim to combat attack techniques that abuse the "npm install" command to trigger the execution of malicious code...

item.title: Title: Network issues in Saskatoon and Calgary
item.description: Jun 11, 04:30 UTC Resolved - Cloudflare may have experienced issues with network performance in Saskatoon and Calgary between 04:20 - 04:45 UTC. The incident is now resolved

item.title: From Explicit Elements to Implicit Intent: A Predefined Library for Auditable Behavioral Inference
item.description: arXiv:2606.11207v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present SemantiClean, a modular framework for extracting structured semantic signals from e-commerce session data and driving pluggable inference targets including purchase intent, customer segmentation, and product affinity through a shared...

item.title: Position: Hippocampal Explicit Memory Is the Cornerstone for AGI
item.description: arXiv:2606.11245v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated remarkable capabilities across various tasks, raising expectations for Artificial General Intelligence (AGI). This position paper argues that integrating explicit memory is the cornerstone for advancing...

item.title: Can AI Agents Synthesize Scientific Conclusions?
item.description: arXiv:2606.11337v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Scientific AI agents increasingly retrieve evidence, reason across sources, and synthesize conclusions used in consequential decisions. Yet, their ability to do so in high-stakes domains such as health remains unclear. We introduce SciConBench, a...

item.title: Knowing When to Ask: Self-Gated Clarification for Hierarchical Language Agents
item.description: arXiv:2606.11349v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In hierarchical reasoning, failures often originate at intermediate decision points where the agent commits to a wrong branch without recognizing that it lacks critical information. Rather than treating clarification as an external uncertainty...

item.title: Automated Mediator for Human Negotiation: Pre-Mediation via a Structured LLM Pipeline
item.description: arXiv:2606.11379v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Pre-mediation, the preparatory phase preceding direct human negotiation, plays a critical role in achieving mutually beneficial agreements, yet is often omitted due to cost, time, and limited access to trained mediators. We introduce an automated...

item.title: Restless bandits with imperfect binary feedback: PCL-indexability analysis and computation
item.description: arXiv:2606.11192v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We study restless bandits with binary latent states and imperfect binary feedback, motivated by opportunistic spectrum access with sensing errors. For the associated belief-state model, we develop a partial conservation laws (PCL)-based analytical and...

item.title: To Intervene or Not: Guiding Inference-time Alignment with Probabilistic Model Blending
item.description: arXiv:2606.11201v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The wide deployment of LLMs has made model alignment necessary to make newly trained models safely and effectively respond to user instructions. Among different methods, inference-time alignment is often cheaper as it intervenes (i.e., offers...

item.title: Dual-Stance Evaluation of Sycophancy: The Structure of Agreement and the Limits of Intervention
item.description: arXiv:2606.11205v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Activation steering can shift LLM behaviour, but standard evaluations do not typically test whether a sycophancy-reduction direction also suppresses agreement with factually correct statements. We introduce dual-stance evaluation, which tests both...

item.title: Few-Shot Resampling for Scalable Statistically-Sound Data Mining
item.description: arXiv:2606.11235v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: A key step in knowledge discovery is the evaluation of data mining results. In several applications, including pattern mining, graph analysis, and others, this step includes the evaluation of the statistical significance of the results, to avoid...

item.title: ProHiFlo: Hierarchical Flow Matching with Functional Guidance for De Novo Protein Generation
item.description: arXiv:2606.11243v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: De novo protein generation has transformative potential in therapeutic design, enzyme engineering, and synthetic biology. While diffusion-based and flow matching approaches have achieved progress, they typically operate at single resolution and lack...

item.title: PoQ-Judge: A Multi-Architecture Evaluation Framework for Cost-Aware Proof-of-Quality in Decentralized LLM Inference
item.description: arXiv:2606.11196v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Decentralized LLM inference networks need lightweight, reference-free quality evaluation for Proof of Quality (PoQ). We present PoQ-Judge, a framework that trains dedicated judge models to score query-output pairs without ground-truth references. We...

item.title: The Structural Attention Tax: How Retrieval Format Hijacks In-Context Learning Independent of Content
item.description: arXiv:2606.11198v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems inject external knowledge to improve LLM outputs, yet the format of injected content -- distinct from its semantic relevance -- can independently distort the model's attention distribution. We identify and...

item.title: NightFeats @ MMU-RAGent NeurIPS 2025: A Context-Optimized Multi-Agent RAG System for the Text-to-Text Track
item.description: arXiv:2606.11199v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present NightFeats, a structured multi-agent retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) system submitted to the MMU-RAGent competition at NeurIPS 2025, where it was awarded Best Dynamic Evaluation in the text-to-text track. Rather than targeting...

item.title: Detecting AI-Generated Content on Social Media with Multi-modal Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2606.11200v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Generative AI has enabled the creation of photorealistic images and videos that are increasingly disseminated on social media, often used for spam, misinformation, manipulation, and fraud. Existing AI-generated content (AIGC) detection methods face...

item.title: One Jailbreak, Many Tongues: Learning Language-Insensitive Intention Representations for Multilingual Jailbreak Detection
item.description: arXiv:2606.11202v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed in applications for global multilingual users, yet safety training remains concentrated in dominant languages and has not progressed in parallel with multilingual capability, creating exploitable...

item.title: [AINews] Open Models, Model Labs vs Agent Labs, and What's Untrainable — Sarah Guo
item.description: a quiet day lets us reflect on a great essay

item.title: Image Resizing on R2 issues
item.description: Jun 11, 01:43 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jun 11, 01:31 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 10, 23:46 UTC Identified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. Jun 10, 23:44 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare has...

item.title: INC20000032
item.description: Jun 11, 00:30 UTC Resolved - Current status: We identified and reverted a misconfigured change, and monitored the environment to confirm that service was restored. If you experience additional issues or have questions, please open a support case via Snowflake Community or the Support page in...

item.title: Profiling in PyTorch (Part 2): From nn.Linear to a Fused MLP
Pages Build & Deployment Delays
item.description: Jun 10, 23:46 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jun 10, 22:18 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare Pages customers may be experiencing delays in builds, project creation, or deployments.

item.title: Anatomy of Post-Training: Using Interpretability to Characterize Data and Shape the Learning Signal
item.description: Language-model post-training is the main stage at which model behavior is shaped, yet it still largely involves optimization of scalar rewards that summarize diverse desiderata. This abstraction gives practitioners little visibility into what their data actually teaches models, allowing spurious...

item.title: DiffusionGemma: 4x faster text generation
MLT-Dedup: Efficient Large-Scale Online Video Deduplication via Multi-Level Representations and Spatial-Temporal Matching
item.description: The explosive growth of user-generated video content on online platforms is accompanied by the emergence of numerous near-duplicate videos--videos that are identical or highly similar but differ by partial edits. These duplicates degrade user experience and increase storage and bandwidth costs,...

item.title: Authentication issues related to API requests
item.description: Jun 10, 15:27 UTC Update - We are investigating issues related to sporadic authentication failures impacting approximately 15% of API traffic. We will continue to investigate and provide updates. Jun 10, 15:27 UTC Update - Issues is experiencing degraded performance. We are continuing to...
